notes: Pinned OCI SDK contract: Dataset exposes CreateDataset, GetDataset, ListDatasets, UpdateDataset, and DeleteDataset with lifecycle states CREATING, UPDATING, ACTIVE, NEEDS_ATTENTION, DELETING, DELETED, and FAILED. Create and delete responses expose opc-work-request-id headers, while UpdateDataset returns only the Dataset body.

Pinned OCI SDK contract: CreateDatasetDetails includes compartmentId, annotationFormat, polymorphic datasetSourceDetails, polymorphic datasetFormatDetails, labelSet, optional displayName, description, initialRecordGenerationConfiguration, initialImportDatasetConfiguration, labelingInstructions, freeformTags, and definedTags. UpdateDatasetDetails narrows in-place mutation to displayName, description, labelingInstructions, freeformTags, and definedTags.

Pinned provider/resource facts: the provider surface for oci_data_labeling_service_dataset also includes auxiliary mutators ChangeDatasetCompartment, AddDatasetLabels, RemoveDatasetLabels, RenameDatasetLabels, GenerateDatasetRecords, ImportPreAnnotatedData, and SnapshotDataset. The reviewed controller-backed runtime does not call those helpers and instead publishes only the base CRUD mutation surface from UpdateDataset.

Repo-authored bind-versus-create stays explicit: when no OCI identifier is tracked, safe reuse requires exact compartmentId, annotationFormat, and displayName matching from ListDatasets plus a reusable lifecycle state, and the runtime skips list reuse altogether when displayName is empty.

Repo-authored mutation policy stays explicit: only displayName, description, labelingInstructions, freeformTags, and definedTags reconcile in place. Create-time dataset structure such as annotationFormat, datasetSourceDetails, datasetFormatDetails, labelSet, and initial import or generation configuration remains unsupported post-create drift for the published runtime.

Repo-authored async handling stays lifecycle-based: the reviewed generatedruntime client records shared request/work-request breadcrumbs when OCI returns them and uses read-after-write plus confirm-delete rereads instead of service-local work-request polling.
